channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Anthropic debuts AI agents that save coders extra keystrokes
item.description: Anthropic, a startup backed by Alphabet and Amazon.com, released a pair of updated artificial intelligence models on Tuesday, along with a new capability to autonomously perform computer tasks and save users keystrokes.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic gives its AI models limited ability to control your computer
item.description: Anthropic is giving its new Claude 3.5 Sonnet model the ability to control a user’s computer and access the internet. The move marks a major step in generative AI models’ capabilities—and raises questions about AI companies’ ability to properly mitigate the risks of more autonomous AI.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Here’s the biggest problem with Amazon Prime Video—and how it could be fixed
item.description: October 1 is the official kickoff of spooky-movie season, but this year, we simply could not wait. On the last day of September, my wife and I watched the 2013 throwback slasher, You’re Next, one of many horror movies available to stream that I’d compiled into a seasonal list.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents increase their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Parry, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: IBM launches platform to protect data from AI and quantum risks
item.description: IBM has rolled out a new platform to help enterprise customers better handle the complicated data workflows and security risks that arise when managing AI deployments, hybrid cloud infrastructure, and quantum computing systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: We need a national STEM Act to stem the risk of a declining America
item.description: Wars have unintended consequences, and positive results can emerge from tragedy. For proof, just look at the list from NATO of some unexpected outcomes of R&D that led to society-changing benefits.
item.dc:creator: Frederic Bertley, Fast Company

item.title: Flying air taxis move closer to US takeoff with issuing of FAA rule
item.description: Federal regulators are giving a strong push to electric-powered air taxis by issuing a final rule for operating the aircraft and how pilots will be trained to fly them.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The iPad mini is still the best small tablet around
item.description: We waited three years for Apple to update the iPad mini, and what we got is more of the same — boosted with a much better processor. Apple’s iPad mini has consistently been positioned as either the most versatile, or the most underpowered tablet in the company’s lineup.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Giant fish thought to be extinct is spotted in the Mekong River
item.description: Scientists say a giant fish in the Mekong River thought to be extinct has been spotted three times in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Turismo de Andalucía impulsa la transformación digital de la industria en la región
item.description: La Consejería de Turismo de la Junta de Andalucía ha suscrito un acuerdo de colaboración con Microsoft para acelerar la modernización tecnológica del sector turístico de la región.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Aragón destina 6,7 millones de euros a la puesta en marcha de su Plan de Innovación
item.description: Aragón da un paso adelante y pone en marcha por primera vez un Plan de Innovación con el que posicionarse como un referente en competitividad tecnológica.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Biden administration awards $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for Michigan factory
item.description: The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Perplexity is sued by Dow Jones and ‘New York Post’ for copyright infringement
item.description: Rupert Murdoch’s Dow Jones, which is the parent company of the Wall Street Journal, and the New York Post both sued AI search startup Perplexity on Monday for infringing copyrighted content. The News Corp.-owned companies accused Perplexity of a “massive amount of illegal copying” of their work.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: Still using 'LOL'? That could mean you are getting old
item.description: As time has seen several follow-up iterations, including ROFL and LMAO, the original "laugh out loud" acronym is a telltale sign that who you are talking to may be a millennial.
item.dc:creator: Juan Cisneros, NewsNation

item.title: One of the world’s largest book publishers adds AI warnings to its books
item.description: To make generative AI tools (genAI) work as well as possible, tech companies have chosen to train their large language models (LLMs) on large amounts of text, even though doing so could run afoul of copyright laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft to launch autonomous AI agents in November
item.description: Microsoft will soon let customers build autonomous AI agents that can be configured to perform complex tasks with little or no input from humans.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: The federal government plans to provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor to produce a key material for computer chips, an announcement Monday with possible political significance as the new factory would be in the swing state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap
item.description: Addressing the federal cybersecurity skills gap requires a multifaceted approach in coordination with the private sector. The post Top considerations for narrowing the federal cybersecurity skills gap first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: GoFundMe bets social media can unlock Gen Z giving. Meta partnership, new tools will test that
item.description: “We play a really important role helping people ask for help and give help in the world,” GoFundMe CEO Tim Cadogan said.
item.dc:creator: JAMES POLLARD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Army aims for speed in new $10B software contract
item.description: Potential bidders have until the end of this week to offer comments on the Army's latest draft request for proposals. 15-20 firms will win spots on the contract The post Army aims for speed in new $10B software contract first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jared Serbu, Federal News Network

item.title: Buyer’s guide: Edge data centers
item.description: Edge data centers include hardware, software, applications, data management, connectivity, gateways, security, and advanced analytics. They come in all shapes and sizes depending on the use case: Small ones can fit into a wiring closet. Larger ones resemble traditional data centers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Stopping the rot in AI spending
item.description: They say that half the money spent on advertising is wasted, but the problem is figuring out which half. If that’s true, the problem is arguably worse for AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot could be the future of affordable in-home care
item.description: A humanoid robot made by a Chinese company proposes to redefine personalized elderly care and assistance for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Get your nominations in for the Oscars of federal technology transfers
item.description: "Technology's changed quite a bit since the 80s. In 2024, we had 32 award winners we recognized from eight federal agencies," said Jesse Midgett. The post Get your nominations in for the Oscars of federal technology transfers first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: US agency adopts rule to make it easier for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions
item.description: The Federal Trade Commission adopted a final rule Wednesday that will require businesses to make it easy for consumers to cancel unwanted subscriptions and memberships.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Survey: 71% of women in IT work long hours to climb ladder
item.description: Nearly three-fourths of women in IT said they work longer hours to improve their chances of career advancement, due in part to gender bias and discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Elon Musk unveils Tesla's Robovan, Robotaxi, humanoid robots
item.description: Tesla's "We, Robot" event reveals the Robovan, Robotaxi and the Optimus humanoid robot. Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson examines the company's latest innovations in autonomous technology.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
